subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 - Player-Count Audit
- **Reference list** — The rulebook defines a single supported count: 2 players only.
- **Variation check** — No setup/resource/rules tables for 3+ players are present.
- **Scaling rules** — No fewer/more player adjustments are listed.
- **Mode check** — No solo mode is listed.

--- 2 Players ---
#### Boards and markers
- **Quilt boards** — 2 total (1 per player).
- **Time board** — 1 central time board (double-sided art only).
- **Time tokens** — 2 total (lime green and yellow).
- **Neutral token** — 1 neutral token.
- **Special tile** — 1 special tile (worth 7 points).

#### Patches
- **Regular patches** — 33 total.
- **Special leather patches** — 5 total.

#### Buttons (currency/points)
- **1-button tiles** — 32.
- **5-button tiles** — 12.
- **10-button tiles** — 5.
- **20-button tiles** — 1.
- **Supply rule** — Keep all remaining buttons in shared supply within reach.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
#### Setup sequence
1. **Center board** — Place the time board in the middle.
2. **Start positions** — Put both time tokens on the starting space.
3. **Leather patches** — Place all 5 special patches on the marked time-board spaces.
4. **Special tile** — Place the special 7-point tile nearby.
5. **Patch market ring** — Arrange all 33 regular patches face up in a circle/oval around the time board.
6. **Neutral token anchor** — Find the smallest patch (size 1x2).
7. **Neutral token placement** — Put the neutral token between that 1x2 patch and the next patch clockwise.

#### Quick check before play
- **Patch access rule** — Current buyer may only take from the 3 patches clockwise in front of neutral token.
- **Shared supply rule** — Button tiles stay in one common supply.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
- **Board** — Each player takes 1 quilt board.
- **Token** — Each player takes 1 time token.
- **Starting currency** — Each player takes exactly 5 buttons.
- **Empty quilt start** — No regular patches start on either quilt board.
- **Shared buttons** — All unclaimed button tiles remain in common supply.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
- **First player** — The player who last used a needle starts.
- **Core timing rule** — Turns are not strictly alternating.
- **Who plays next** — The player furthest behind on the time board takes the next turn.
- **Tie on same space** — The token on top takes the next turn.
- **Consecutive turns** — One player may take multiple turns in a row if still behind.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
#### Turn sequence (always in order)
1. **Identify active player** — Furthest-behind time token takes the turn.
2. **Choose action** — Take exactly one action: A or B.
3. **Move time token** — Advance according to chosen action.
4. **Resolve passed spaces** — Trigger each time-board event moved onto or past.
5. **Recheck initiative** — Compare token positions to determine next player.

#### Action A (Advance and Receive Buttons)
1. **Destination** — Move to the space directly in front of opponent token.
2. **Income** — Gain 1 button per space actually moved.

#### Action B (Take and Place a Patch)
1. **Patch selection** — Choose 1 of exactly 3 available patches in front of neutral token (clockwise).
2. **Neutral movement** — Move neutral token to sit next to chosen patch.
3. **Payment** — Pay printed button cost to supply.
4. **Placement** — Place patch on your quilt board without overlap.
5. **Time cost** — Move your time token by printed time value on that patch.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
#### A) Advance and Receive Buttons
- **Move target** — You must stop directly in front of opponent token.
- **Button gain** — Gain 1 button per space actually moved.
- **End-board cap** — If movement would pass final space, stop on final space.
- **Cap income** — At end-board cap, gain buttons only for spaces actually moved.

#### B) Take and Place a Patch
- **Selection limit** — You may choose only from 3 patches in front of neutral token.
- **Cost** — Pay the chosen patch's printed button cost.
- **Placement rule** — Patch pieces may not overlap any placed patch.
- **Orientation freedom** — You may turn the patch before placing.
- **Time payment** — Move your time token by patch's printed time cost.

#### Universal restrictions
- **One action per turn** — You must choose either A or B, not both.
- **Same-space stacking** — If tokens land on same space, the arriving token goes on top.
- **Personal board only** — You place patches only on your own quilt board.

#### Button handling
- **Currency role** — Buttons buy patches.
- **Scoring role** — Buttons also count as endgame points.
- **Supply source** — All button gains come from shared button supply.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
#### Time-board triggers
- **Trigger timing** — Resolve events whenever you move onto or past marked spaces.
- **Special patch event** — Take 1 leather patch immediately and place it immediately.
- **Single-space note** — Leather patches are the only way to fill 1-square holes.
- **Button-income event** — Gain buttons equal to all button-income icons on your placed patches.

#### Special tile race (7x7)
- **Condition** — First player to fully fill any 7x7 area on their quilt board takes the special tile.
- **Value** — Special tile is worth +7 points at game end.

#### Tempo/initiative system
- **Behind-player priority** — Being behind grants the next turn.
- **Multi-turn windows** — Low-time actions can create back-to-back turns.
- **Tradeoff** — Time cost is both a penalty and a turn-order lever.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
#### End trigger
- **Game end condition** — End game when both time tokens reach the last time-board space.
- **Overshoot handling** — If movement would pass last space, token stops on last space.

#### Final score formula
1. **Base points** — Count all buttons you have left.
2. **Special tile bonus** — Add +7 if you own the special tile.
3. **Empty-space penalty** — Subtract 2 points for each empty quilt-board square.

#### Winner and tie-break
- **Winner** — Higher final score wins.
- **Tie-break** — If tied, player who reached final time-board space first wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
#### First turns (practical focus)
- **Income first** — Buy at least one early patch with button-income icons before midgame income triggers.
- **Shape discipline** — Favor placements that keep center and edges flexible for later polyominoes.
- **Hole prevention** — Avoid creating isolated 1x1 holes unless you are confident about leather-patch access.
- **Cash buffer** — Keep enough buttons to buy a strong patch if it enters your 3-patch window.
- **Tempo setup** — Use low-time picks to preserve possible consecutive turns when market position matters.

#### Opening benchmark
- **By early-mid game** — Aim to have both workable board geometry and an income engine, not just one.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
- **Cheap-trap buying** — Taking low-cost but awkward shapes often causes larger empty-space penalties later.
- **Income neglect** — Ignoring button-income icons leads to weak buying power in the middle game.
- **1x1 overproduction** — Creating many isolated single squares is punished unless you secure leather patches.
- **Zero-button turns** — Spending to zero can lock you out of critical patches on the next swing.
- **Alternation assumption** — Treating turn order as alternating misplays the time-track initiative system.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
--- 2 Players ---
#### Decision 1: Economy vs fit
- **Income pick** — Take stronger button-income even if shape is awkward.
- **Fit pick** — Take cleaner shape even if income is weaker.
- **Decision test** — Compare next income trigger timing vs expected hole penalties.

#### Decision 2: Tempo vs patch quality
- **Tempo line** — Take lower-time patch to keep initiative and possibly chain turns.
- **Quality line** — Take higher-time premium patch and concede initiative.
- **Decision test** — Evaluate whether opponent can capture a key patch before your next action.

#### Decision 3: 7x7 race vs global board health
- **Race line** — Push for special tile (+7) by concentrating fill pattern.
- **Balance line** — Keep whole-board efficiency and avoid dead zones.
- **Decision test** — If race creates multiple unavoidable holes, +7 may not compensate.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for scan-first usability with explicit player-count audit and per-section 2-player headers from rulebook-supported count.
